symptoms.symptom_name: New skin breakdown / superficial ulceration at the residual-limb end

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A skin breach is the portal of entry and the anchor finding for the infection.
Skin breakdown does not by itself explain pain felt in the missing limb.

symptoms.symptom_name: Surrounding erythema and warmth larger than the ulcer

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Peri-wound erythema and warmth are the defining clinical features of cellulitis.
Warmth with an expanding erythematous border beyond the friction site points to infection rather than pure dermatitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Rapid escalation over 4 hours after a day of prosthetic friction

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Rapid worsening in a compromised limb raises concern for progressive soft-tissue infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe burning/cramping phantom limb pain refractory to exhausted analgesia

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Phantom limb pain flare / neuropathic pain crisis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Burning, cramping pain localised to the absent limb is the hallmark of phantom limb pain.
Infection can amplify local pain but the phantom character is neuropathic; the two coexist.

symptoms.symptom_name: Exquisite focal point tenderness at one spot on the lateral stump

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A discrete Tinel-positive point of exquisite tenderness is characteristic of a stump neuroma.
A low-grade temperature signals early systemic response to infection; patient-obtainable so admissible in telehealth, unverified.

symptoms.symptom_name: No high fever, no confusion, no purulent discharge, redness not ascending (pertinent negatives)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of systemic toxicity and ascending erythema argues against emergency-level infection now, supporting same-day rather than emergency escalation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Unable to bear weight; wheelchair-bound with home trip hazards (falls risk)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Falls risk (safety issue, not a diagnosis)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Loss of mobility plus dim, cluttered environment materially raises fall risk in an older amputee.

symptoms.symptom_name: No crepitus or fluctuance on palpation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of crepitus/fluctuance lowers the likelihood of gas-forming or collected infection — but requires hands-on examination that telehealth cannot provide.

symptoms.symptom_name: Contralateral calf without swelling or tenderness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No calf swelling or tenderness reduces the probability of DVT as the pain driver.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Early cellulitis of a compromised residual limb
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Skin breach + expanding peri-wound erythema/warmth + rapid escalation + low-grade temp in an amputated limb is the cluster that must not be overshadowed by the phantom-pain story.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person wound examination with erythema-border marking
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Depth, fluctuance, crepitus and true erythema extent can only be judged hands-on; anchors the antibiotic and admission decision.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Wound swab for microscopy, culture and sensitivities
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Guides targeted antibiotic therapy for the stump infection.
Quantifies systemic inflammatory response and provides a baseline to track progression.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Plain radiograph / MRI of the residual limb if osteomyelitis suspected
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Considered if the wound probes to bone, fails to improve, or systemic markers rise — not first-line at this visit.
No clinical features of DVT; ordering it routinely here is an unnecessary investigation that delays wound care.
